records.ety: [OE. manicle, OF. manicle, F. manicle sort glove, manacle, L. manicula a little hand, dim. of manus hand; cf. L. manica sleeve, manacle, fr. manus. See Manual.]

defs: A handcuff; a shackle for the hand or wrist; -- usually in the plural.
quotes: Doctrine unto fools is as fetters on the feet, and like manacles on the right hand.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Manacled (mn"*k'ld); p. pr. & vb. n. Manacling (?).]

defs: To put handcuffs or other fastening upon, for confining the hands; to shackle; to confine; to restrain from the use of the limbs or natural powers.
quotes: Is it thus you use this monarch, to manacle and shackle him hand and foot ?
